expectations.text: Information limiting: Does not dump all features at once; presents 3-5 key items first and offers to expand
expectations.evidence: Dumps all features in 3 large tables (18+ items total) plus tech stack (11 layers), data model, user flows, revenue model, competitive analysis, and 12-week roadmap in a single response.

expectations.text: Positional anchoring: New concepts are introduced in relation to existing structure
expectations.evidence: Sections are listed sequentially but new concepts (tech stack, revenue model, competitive analysis) appear without connecting to what the user asked about.

expectations.text: Must-have vs nice-to-have: Explicitly separates MVP-essential from later features
expectations.evidence: Does separate MVP (Phase 1) from Phase 2 with priority rankings. This is a strength of the response.

expectations.text: State file created with correct schema
expectations.evidence: No state file created (baseline has no skill).

expectations.text: Does not overwhelm: Response feels structured and digestible
expectations.evidence: 257 lines, 10 major sections. A classic information dump that would cause the exact cognitive overload this skill aims to prevent.
